page.title: Paraphrase and Reformulation
page.subtitle_html: Paraphrase and reformulation - C2 precise restatement, clarification, and formal reframing.
page.subtitle_text: C2 precise restatement, clarification, and formal reframing.

blocks.body: {"level":"C2","intro":"Paraphrase and reformulation: compact anchors for restating an argument with greater precision or a different emphasis.","rules":[{"label":"Clarifying restatement","text":"Use in other words, that is to say, and put differently when the second clause sharpens the first.","example":"In other words, the problem lies in a lack of coordination."},{"label":"Reframing","text":"Use reformulation to change focus without changing the core meaning.","example":"The real issue lies not in speed but in quality."},{"label":"Formal precision","text":"Avoid vague repetition; make the new formulation add structure, evidence, or contrast.","example":"This can be understood as a failure of planning rather than execution."}]}

blocks.heading: C2 focus
blocks.body: This compact page anchors the Polyglot English C2 lesson on paraphrase and reformulation.
